GAME MASTER INTEGRATION\r\n\r\nDo not fully redesign the Game Master dashboard in this phase, but extend it enough to drive and validate the new Player Companion sections.\r\n\r\nAdd only the controls necessary to support development testing of:\r\n\r\n* Reveal map location\r\n* Reveal route segment\r\n* Award artifact\r\n* Reveal artifact silhouette\r\n* Connect artifacts\r\n* Discover side quest\r\n* Update side-quest objective\r\n* Complete side quest\r\n* Add journal annotation\r\n* Add player-facing log event\r\n* Tease Finale\r\n* Update generic Finale requirement\r\n* Clear unseen indicators where appropriate\r\n* Reset development campaign preset\r\n\r\nAll controls must:\r\n\r\n* Require authentication\r\n* Use confirmation where destructive or progression-changing\r\n* Create domain events\r\n* Create audit entries\r\n* Be idempotent\r\n* Update the player live\r\n* Support undo where appropriate\r\n\r\nDo not add fake client-only buttons.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 45. DATABASE AND MIGRATIONS\r\n\r\nExtend the database only where necessary.\r\n\r\nAny schema changes must include:\r\n\r\n* Migration\r\n* Foreign keys\r\n* Unique constraints\r\n* Indexes\r\n* Appropriate enums or validated strings\r\n* Timestamps\r\n* Cascade behavior review\r\n* Seed updates\r\n* Test coverage\r\n* Documentation\r\n\r\nReview whether current schema already supports the new systems before adding tables.\r\n\r\nAvoid redundant tables.\r\n\r\nAvoid storing derived display state when it can be reliably computed.\r\n\r\nUse snapshots where performance or reconciliation requires them.\r\n\r\nDocument any new schema decisions.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 46. API AND SERVER VALIDATION\r\n\r\nAll content APIs must enforce release boundaries.\r\n\r\nTest that:\r\n\r\n* Locked chapter content is not returned.\r\n* Hidden map coordinates are not returned.\r\n* Hidden artifact details are not returned.\r\n* Hidden side-quest content is not returned.\r\n* Finale content is not returned.\r\n* Internal Game Master notes are not returned.\r\n* Audit information is not returned.\r\n* Invalid campaign tokens fail.\r\n* Cross-campaign access fails.\r\n* Player cannot enumerate campaigns.\r\n* Player cannot mutate campaign state.\r\n* Replay requests do not mutate progression.\r\n\r\nUse centralized server-side authorization and visibility filtering.\r\n\r\nDo not rely on client components to hide protected fields.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 47. VISUAL DESIGN QUALITY\r\n\r\nMaintain and extend the established design system.\r\n\r\nThe new sections should share:\r\n\r\n* Parchment language\r\n* Brass language\r\n* Ocean-dark background\r\n* Moonlit magical accent\r\n* Muted crimson thread\r\n* Consistent shadows\r\n* Consistent texture intensity\r\n* Consistent corner and edge treatments\r\n* Consistent motion timing\r\n* Consistent typography\r\n* Consistent focus states\r\n\r\nEach section may have distinct personality:\r\n\r\n* Journal: ink and parchment\r\n* Chart: cartography and fog\r\n* Altar: wood, brass, and moonlight\r\n* Ledger: secret notes and constellations\r\n* Log: chronological captainâ€™s record\r\n* Finale: dormant celestial mechanism\r\n\r\nBut the application must still feel unified.\r\n\r\nAvoid:\r\n\r\n* Excess gradients\r\n* Neon fantasy styling\r\n* Generic glassmorphism\r\n* Excess glowing borders\r\n* Flat card grids\r\n* Decorative clutter\r\n* Poor contrast\r\n* Tiny cursive body text\r\n* Oversized headings that consume mobile screens\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 48. ANIMATION DESIGN\r\n\r\nAll new animations must use documented motion tokens and the existing orchestrator where appropriate.\r\n\r\nCreate reusable animation primitives for:\r\n\r\n* Section transition\r\n* Page turn\r\n* Map marker reveal\r\n* Route drawing\r\n* Artifact award\r\n* Artifact connection\r\n* Side-quest discovery\r\n* Ledger note reveal\r\n* Log entry arrival\r\n* Finale tease\r\n* Unseen indicator\r\n* Objective update\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n\r\n* Reduced-motion variants\r\n* Skip behavior for ceremonies\r\n* No stuck intermediate states\r\n* No overlap\r\n* No layout thrashing\r\n* No animation-only meaning\r\n* No excessive duration\r\n* No motion sickness\r\n* Deterministic test support\r\n\r\nDo not implement every interaction as a cinematic event.\r\n\r\nRoutine navigation should remain fast.\r\n\r\nSave theatrical treatment for meaningful progression.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 49. AUDIO DESIGN\r\n\r\nExtend audio only where it improves important events.\r\n\r\nPossible temporary sounds:\r\n\r\n* Page turn\r\n* Map ink scratch\r\n* Compass click\r\n* Artifact placement\r\n* Brass mechanism\r\n* Side-quest whisper\r\n* Log quill\r\n* Finale chain tension\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n\r\n* Properly licensed or original\r\n* Documented in asset licenses\r\n* Optional\r\n* Volume-controlled\r\n* No autoplay violation\r\n* Graceful failure\r\n* Visual equivalent\r\n* No sudden loud playback\r\n* No ripped game audio\r\n\r\nDo not add constant loud ambience.\r\n\r\nAmbient sound should remain subtle.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 50. TESTING STRATEGY\r\n\r\nExpand automated coverage.\r\n\r\n## Unit tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* Chapter visibility filtering\r\n* Map visibility filtering\r\n* Artifact visibility filtering\r\n* Side-quest visibility filtering\r\n* Finale visibility filtering\r\n* Event-to-log transformation\r\n* Unseen-state logic\r\n* Objective derivation\r\n* Artifact state transitions\r\n* Side-quest state transitions\r\n* Map state transitions\r\n* Navigation-state restoration\r\n* Preference persistence\r\n* Reduced-motion branching\r\n* Replay nonmutation\r\n* Locked-content serialization\r\n\r\n## Integration tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* Campaign snapshot includes only released content\r\n* Event processing updates correct section\r\n* Game Master control creates domain event\r\n* Event updates snapshot\r\n* Player receives event\r\n* Undo restores prior state\r\n* Duplicate event ignored\r\n* Cross-device viewed-state behavior\r\n* Database migration integrity\r\n* Seed preset integrity\r\n\r\n## End-to-end tests\r\n\r\nTest complete workflows:\r\n\r\n1. Player opens Journal.\r\n2. Player navigates all available sections.\r\n3. Locked sections remain protected.\r\n4. Game Master reveals map location.\r\n5. Player receives map reveal.\r\n6. Game Master awards artifact.\r\n7. Player receives artifact ceremony.\r\n8. Game Master discovers side quest.\r\n9. Player receives ledger update.\r\n10. Shipâ€™s Log receives correct entries.\r\n11. Finale changes from hidden to sealed teaser.\r\n12. Refresh preserves all state.\r\n13. New browser receives correct snapshot.\r\n14. Replay does not mutate progression.\r\n15. Undo works.\r\n16. Offline recovery works.\r\n17. Mobile navigation works.\r\n18. Reduced motion works.\r\n19. Keyboard navigation works.\r\n20. Locked content never appears in network responses.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 51. RESPONSIVE VIEWPORT VALIDATION\r\n\r\nCapture and inspect at minimum:\r\n\r\n## Desktop\r\n\r\n* 2560Ã—1440\r\n* 1920Ã—1080\r\n* 1440Ã—900\r\n\r\n## Mobile\r\n\r\n* 390Ã—844\r\n* 430Ã—932\r\n* 844Ã—390\r\n\r\nUse Chromium and WebKit where practical.\r\n\r\nCapture each major section at relevant states:\r\n\r\n* Journal active\r\n* Journal locked chapter index\r\n* Chart with revealed route\r\n* Chart empty\r\n* Artifact altar awarded state\r\n* Artifact inspection\r\n* Side-quest active\r\n* Side-quest empty\r\n* Shipâ€™s Log populated\r\n* Shipâ€™s Log empty\r\n* Finale sealed\r\n* Objective expanded\r\n* Mobile navigation\r\n* Fullscreen Companion Mode\r\n* Reduced-motion state\r\n\r\nDo not merely generate screenshots.\r\n\r\nInspect and correct:\r\n\r\n* Overflow\r\n* Cropping\r\n* Weak hierarchy\r\n* Tiny controls\r\n* Empty dead space\r\n* Excessive scrolling\r\n* Poor line length\r\n* Unreadable texture\r\n* Bottom-navigation overlap\r\n* Safe-area errors\r\n* Map gesture conflicts\r\n* Broken artifact scaling\r\n* Finale composition\r\n* Objective obstruction\r\n\r\nPerform at least two deliberate visual refinement passes.\r\n\r\nDocument issues and corrections.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 52. REALISTIC CONTENT STRESS TESTS\r\n\r\nTest components with:\r\n\r\n* Very long chapter title\r\n* Very short chapter title\r\n* Long narrative\r\n* Short narrative\r\n* Long riddle\r\n* Multiple hints\r\n* No hints\r\n* Many artifacts\r\n* One artifact\r\n* Long artifact description\r\n* Many side quests\r\n* No side quests\r\n* Long Shipâ€™s Log\r\n* Simultaneous unseen updates\r\n* Multiple events in rapid sequence\r\n* Missing optional image\r\n* Failed audio asset\r\n* Slow network\r\n* Offline reconnect\r\n* Narrow landscape phone\r\n* Large desktop\r\n\r\nDo not design only around ideal seed content.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 53. BROWSER AND NETWORK VALIDATION\r\n\r\nInspect:\r\n\r\n* Browser console\r\n* Server logs\r\n* Network requests\r\n* Event stream\r\n* Failed assets\r\n* Hydration warnings\r\n* React warnings\r\n* Accessibility warnings\r\n* Reconnect loops\r\n* Duplicate API calls\r\n* Unreleased payload leakage\r\n* Slow query behavior\r\n\r\nCorrect material warnings.\r\n\r\nDo not ignore console noise merely because screenshots look pretty.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 54. STARTUP AND DEVELOPER EXPERIENCE\r\n\r\nPreserve the existing one-command startup and validation workflow.\r\n\r\nThe user must still be able to start the entire development environment with the existing simple command.\r\n\r\nIf the new systems require:\r\n\r\n* Additional migrations\r\n* New seed steps\r\n* New generated assets\r\n* New environment variables\r\n\r\nintegrate them into the automated startup process.\r\n\r\nDo not make the user return to a fifteen-command setup ritual.\r\n\r\nUpdate the startup scripts if necessary.\r\n\r\nValidate a clean clone.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 55. CLEAN-CLONE VALIDATION\r\n\r\nCreate a temporary clean clone from GitHub after implementation.\r\n\r\nUsing only committed content:\r\n\r\n1. Install dependencies.\r\n2. Create local environment from `.env.example`.\r\n3. Start the development database.\r\n4. Apply migrations.\r\n5. Load seed data.\r\n6. Start the application.\r\n7. PLAYER COMPANION INFORMATION ARCHITECTURE\r\n\r\nCreate a clear Player Companion information architecture.\r\n\r\nThe major sections must be available through a unified navigation model.\r\n\r\nSuggested logical section identifiers:\r\n\r\n* `journal`\r\n* `chart`\r\n* `treasures`\r\n* `quests`\r\n* `log`\r\n* `finale`\r\n\r\nThe exact route structure may use:\r\n\r\n* Nested routes\r\n* Layout-state navigation\r\n* URL-query state\r\n* Mobile sheet navigation\r\n* A hybrid approach\r\n\r\nHowever:\r\n\r\n* Navigation state should be reflected in the URL where practical.\r\n* Refreshing should preserve the current section.\r\n* Browser back and forward should behave sensibly.\r\n* Deep links must not expose locked content.\r\n* Mobile and desktop should share the same domain state.\r\n* Navigation must not require reloading the entire application.\r\n* Live updates must continue while switching sections.\r\n\r\nDocument the final navigation architecture.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 6. DESKTOP EXPERIENCE\r\n\r\nOn desktop and large secondary monitors, the Player Companion should resemble a layered physical story workspace.\r\n\r\nThe experience should make strong use of:\r\n\r\n* 1920Ã—1080\r\n* 2560Ã—1440\r\n* 1440Ã—900\r\n* Large ultrawide-like available space where practical\r\n\r\nDo not simply center a narrow mobile column.\r\n\r\nCreate a purposeful desktop composition.\r\n\r\nPotential composition:\r\n\r\n* Central primary content region\r\n* Persistent navigation rail or artifact tabs\r\n* Secondary chart or context panel\r\n* Current objective ribbon\r\n* Ambient background depth\r\n* Context-sensitive side content\r\n* Fullscreen-friendly behavior\r\n\r\nThe desktop experience should feel appropriate to leave open on Seraâ€™s second monitor while Sea of Thieves runs on the primary monitor.\r\n\r\nRequired desktop qualities:\r\n\r\n* Active clue readable at a glance\r\n* Current objective always accessible\r\n* Major sections reachable in one interaction\r\n* No tiny decorative controls\r\n* No excessive scrolling for core information\r\n* No giant empty dead zones\r\n* No cluttered admin-dashboard appearance\r\n* No panels competing equally for attention\r\n* No unnecessary modal dependence\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 7. IPHONE EXPERIENCE\r\n\r\nThe iPhone experience must be intentionally redesigned.\r\n\r\nDo not compress the desktop layout into a narrow viewport.\r\n\r\nUse:\r\n\r\n* Single-column primary reading flow\r\n* Layered parchment pages\r\n* Bottom navigation or another touch-appropriate navigation model\r\n* Large touch targets\r\n* Safe-area insets\r\n* Compact objective access\r\n* Clear back navigation\r\n* Stable scroll behavior\r\n* Mobile-specific transitions\r\n* Separate map, treasure, quest, and log views\r\n* No hover-dependent behavior\r\n* No tiny icon-only controls without labels or accessible names\r\n\r\nSupport:\r\n\r\n* Portrait\r\n* Landscape\r\n* Safari dynamic viewport behavior\r\n* Notch\r\n* Home indicator\r\n* Address-bar expansion and collapse\r\n* Backgrounding and returning\r\n* Text scaling\r\n\r\nThe active clue must remain easy to recover after navigating elsewhere.\r\n\r\nA persistent mobile control such as:\r\n\r\n* Current Objective\r\n* Return to Active Clue\r\n* Continue Tale\r\n\r\nshould remain accessible without obscuring content.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 8. COMPANION NAVIGATION\r\n\r\nCreate a cohesive navigational system that feels like part of the artifact.\r\n\r\nPossible visual metaphors:\r\n\r\n* Brass chapter tabs\r\n* Leather index markers\r\n* Hanging charms\r\n* Compass-selector ring\r\n* Ribbon bookmarks\r\n* Engraved navigation strip\r\n* Sealed page dividers\r\n\r\nDo not use a generic SaaS sidebar.\r\n\r\nNavigation states should include:\r\n\r\n* Available\r\n* Active\r\n* Locked\r\n* Newly updated\r\n* Contains unseen content\r\n* Completed where relevant\r\n\r\nUse restrained notification treatments.\r\n\r\nDo not place bright red badges everywhere.\r\n\r\nThe navigation must support:\r\n\r\n* Keyboard use\r\n* Screen readers\r\n* Touch\r\n* Focus management\r\n* Reduced motion\r\n* Section deep links\r\n* Current section state\r\n* Unseen-update indicators\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 9. PERSISTENT CURRENT OBJECTIVE\r\n\r\nCreate a persistent Current Objective system.\r\n\r\nThis should not be a giant permanent banner consuming the screen.\r\n\r\nThe objective must be:\r\n\r\n* Easy to glance at\r\n* Available in every main section\r\n* Expandable for full clue review\r\n* Collapsible when desired\r\n* Synced to the active chapter\r\n* Updated live\r\n* Hidden or changed appropriately when no chapter is active\r\n* Responsive\r\n* Accessible\r\n\r\nDesktop behavior may use:\r\n\r\n* Ribbon\r\n* Brass-edged objective plate\r\n* Pinned journal strip\r\n* Floating parchment tab\r\n\r\nMobile behavior may use:\r\n\r\n* Bottom sheet\r\n* Compact persistent bar\r\n* Expandable objective drawer\r\n* â€œReturn to Clueâ€ button\r\n\r\nThe Current Objective system must support:\r\n\r\n* Objective title\r\n* Short objective summary\r\n* Active chapter\r\n* Hint availability\r\n* Current location-status text where appropriate\r\n* Return-to-journal action\r\n\r\nDo not expose unreleased objectives.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 10. CAPTAINâ€™S JOURNAL\r\n\r\nThe Captainâ€™s Journal is the central story interface.\r\n\r\nIt must support multiple chapters and all required chapter states.\r\n\r\nAt minimum:\r\n\r\n* LOCKED\r\n* TEASER\r\n* READY\r\n* REVEALING\r\n* ACTIVE\r\n* SOLVED\r\n* COMPLETE\r\n\r\nThe Journal must display:\r\n\r\n* Tale title\r\n* Chapter number\r\n* Chapter title\r\n* Narrative introduction\r\n* Current objective\r\n* Riddle or clue\r\n* Released hints\r\n* Chapter status\r\n* Optional annotations\r\n* Related artifact\r\n* Related map destination\r\n* Related side quest, if discovered\r\n* Completion seal or solved marker\r\n* Previous and next chapter navigation where permitted\r\n\r\nThe Journal must not hardcode development chapters in React components.\r\n\r\nAll chapter content must be data-driven.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 11. JOURNAL CHAPTER INDEX\r\n\r\nCreate a chapter index that supports:\r\n\r\n* Locked chapters\r\n* Teaser chapters\r\n* Active chapter\r\n* Solved chapters\r\n* Completed chapters\r\n* Unseen newly released chapters\r\n* Chapter numbers\r\n* Short safe subtitles where permitted\r\n* Completion seals\r\n* Hidden chapter names when secrecy requires it\r\n\r\nLocked chapters must not reveal:\r\n\r\n* Full titles\r\n* Objectives\r\n* Riddles\r\n* Destinations\r\n* Artifacts\r\n* Final story content\r\n\r\nA locked chapter may show only something like:\r\n\r\n* â€œChapter IVâ€\r\n* â€œThe page remains sealedâ€\r\n* A silhouette\r\n* A symbolic mark\r\n* A safe teaser explicitly released by the Game Master\r\n\r\nThe chapter index should feel like part of a physical book, not a file tree.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 12. JOURNAL READING EXPERIENCE\r\n\r\nCreate distinct desktop and mobile reading behavior.\r\n\r\nDesktop:\r\n\r\n* Open-book or layered-page composition\r\n* Balanced line lengths\r\n* Meaningful margins\r\n* Page texture\r\n* Section hierarchy\r\n* Chapter ornament\r\n* Optional facing-page context\r\n* Persistent but restrained chapter navigation\r\n\r\nMobile:\r\n\r\n* Single-page vertical reading\r\n* Layered parchment cards\r\n* Sticky chapter context where useful\r\n* Clean heading hierarchy\r\n* Readable body text\r\n* Easy return to index\r\n* No two-column book simulation on a narrow phone\r\n\r\nThe journal must remain usable with:\r\n\r\n* 200% browser zoom\r\n* Reduced motion\r\n* Audio disabled\r\n* Keyboard navigation\r\n* Screen reader\r\n* Long chapter text\r\n* Very short chapter text\r\n* Multiple hints\r\n* No hints\r\n* Missing optional content\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 13. HINT SYSTEM\r\n\r\nBuild the Player-facing hint interface.\r\n\r\nHints must be:\r\n\r\n* Data-driven\r\n* Released by Game Master events\r\n* Ordered\r\n* Persisted\r\n* Individually viewable\r\n* Clearly separated from the original clue\r\n* Marked with release timing where appropriate\r\n* Safe from pre-release leakage\r\n\r\nHint states:\r\n\r\n* Unavailable\r\n* Available\r\n* Newly released\r\n* Viewed\r\n\r\nThe player should not accidentally reveal future hints.\r\n\r\nIf hints are automatically made available through Game Master progression, they should appear with a restrained theatrical response.\r\n\r\nAvoid punishment language.\r\n\r\nDo not make Sera feel like she failed by opening a hint.\r\n\r\nPossible presentation:\r\n\r\n* Folded note\r\n* Whisper from the sea\r\n* Navigatorâ€™s annotation\r\n* Added margin writing\r\n* Wax-sealed assistance slip\r\n\r\nTrack viewed state without treating it as failure.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 14. JOURNAL ANNOTATIONS\r\n\r\nSupport story annotations or discovery notes.\r\n\r\nThese may include:\r\n\r\n* Game Master-released margin notes\r\n* Automatic discovery notes\r\n* Map observations\r\n* Artifact connections\r\n* Side-quest discoveries\r\n* Player-facing chapter updates\r\n\r\nAnnotations should:\r\n\r\n* Appear in a consistent visual language\r\n* Include timestamp or sequence context when useful\r\n* Be data-driven\r\n* Not expose internal Game Master metadata\r\n* Support unread indicators\r\n* Persist\r\n* Be accessible\r\n\r\nDo not create freeform player editing unless already explicitly part of the architecture.\r\n\r\nThis phase is primarily read-only for the player.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 15. VOYAGE CHART\r\n\r\nCreate the full Voyage Chart section.\r\n\r\nThis is an evolving custom story map, not a general Sea of Thieves world map.\r\n\r\nIt should represent only the narrative geography needed for the Tall Tale.\r\n\r\nThe chart must support:\r\n\r\n* Hidden regions\r\n* Obscured regions\r\n* Revealed regions\r\n* Current destination\r\n* Completed destinations\r\n* Optional side-quest locations\r\n* Route segments\r\n* Discovery markers\r\n* Symbolic locations\r\n* Map annotations\r\n* Unknown destinations\r\n* Chapter relationships\r\n\r\nThe chart should feel hand-drawn and physically marked over time.\r\n\r\nAvoid directly copying official Sea of Thieves maps or map assets.\r\n\r\nUse original cartographic styling.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 16. MAP DATA MODEL\r\n\r\nExtend the data model where needed to support:\r\n\r\n* Map\r\n* Map region\r\n* Map location\r\n* Location type\r\n* Reveal state\r\n* Reveal event\r\n* Route segment\r\n* Route sequence\r\n* Marker style\r\n* Location chapter association\r\n* Side-quest association\r\n* Safe public label\r\n* Hidden internal label\r\n* Player-visible description\r\n* Exactness level\r\n* Completion state\r\n* Annotation\r\n* Display coordinates\r\n* Mobile display metadata where necessary\r\n\r\nDo not store layout logic entirely in components.\r\n\r\nThe map should be data-driven.\r\n\r\nSupport multiple map-state levels, such as:\r\n\r\n* UNKNOWN\r\n* RUMORED\r\n* REVEALED\r\n* ACTIVE_DESTINATION\r\n* VISITED\r\n* COMPLETED\r\n* SIDE_QUEST\r\n* SECRET\r\n\r\nA locationâ€™s unreleased internal details must remain server-side.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 17. MAP INTERACTIONS\r\n\r\nDesktop interactions may support:\r\n\r\n* Pan\r\n* Controlled zoom\r\n* Marker selection\r\n* Route inspection\r\n* Hover detail where supplementary\r\n* Current-location focus\r\n* Reset view\r\n* Fit revealed voyage\r\n* Keyboard marker traversal\r\n\r\nMobile interactions must support:\r\n\r\n* Touch pan\r\n* Pinch or controlled zoom if reliable\r\n* Marker tap\r\n* Bottom-sheet details\r\n* Reset view\r\n* Return to current destination\r\n* No hover dependence\r\n\r\nDo not allow the player to zoom infinitely into empty parchment.\r\n\r\nUse sensible bounds.\r\n\r\nAvoid interaction patterns that cause accidental browser navigation or unstable page scrolling.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 18. MAP REVEAL THEATRICS\r\n\r\nCreate reusable map-reveal ceremonies.\r\n\r\nPossible effects:\r\n\r\n* Fog withdrawing\r\n* Ink spreading along a route\r\n* Brass compass twitch\r\n* Marker stamped onto parchment\r\n* Red thread connecting locations\r\n* Moonlight exposing hidden lines\r\n* Handwritten annotation appearing\r\n\r\nThese effects must:\r\n\r\n* Use the existing theatrical event orchestrator\r\n* Queue safely\r\n* Respect reduced motion\r\n* Be skippable\r\n* Not replay unintentionally after refresh\r\n* Apply final state even if interrupted\r\n* Perform well on iPhone\r\n* Avoid hundreds of animated elements\r\n* Avoid aggressive camera movement\r\n\r\nMap updates must remain correct even if multiple locations are revealed close together.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 19. TREASURE ALTAR\r\n\r\nCreate the Treasure Altar section.\r\n\r\nThis is where recovered artifacts appear.\r\n\r\nThe visual metaphor should feel like:\r\n\r\n* A captainâ€™s collection table\r\n* Enchanted display altar\r\n* Brass-and-wood reliquary\r\n* Moonlit artifact cabinet\r\n* Physical assembly surface\r\n\r\nAvoid a generic inventory grid.\r\n\r\nArtifacts should feel important.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 20. ARTIFACT DATA MODEL\r\n\r\nSupport:\r\n\r\n* Artifact\r\n* Artifact category\r\n* Artifact title\r\n* Safe hidden title\r\n* Description\r\n* Discovery text\r\n* Visual representation\r\n* Silhouette\r\n* Display position\r\n* Assembly relationship\r\n* Chapter association\r\n* Side-quest association\r\n* Award event\r\n* Inspection content\r\n* Rarity or narrative importance where useful\r\n* Hidden connection metadata\r\n* Finale relationship\r\n* Awarded state\r\n* Viewed state\r\n\r\nArtifacts must be data-driven.\r\n\r\nUnawarded artifacts must not leak their full content to the player.\r\n\r\nA silhouette may be delivered only if explicitly safe.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 21. ARTIFACT STATES\r\n\r\nSupport at minimum:\r\n\r\n* UNKNOWN\r\n* SILHOUETTE\r\n* DISCOVERED\r\n* AWARDED\r\n* INSPECTED\r\n* CONNECTED\r\n* ASSEMBLED\r\n\r\nNot all states need to be used immediately, but the architecture must support them coherently.\r\n\r\nPlayer presentation:\r\n\r\n## Unknown\r\n\r\n* Empty space\r\n* No revealing name\r\n* No description\r\n* Optional mysterious frame\r\n\r\n## Silhouette\r\n\r\n* Safe outline only\r\n* No hidden metadata leakage\r\n* Optional vague category\r\n\r\n## Awarded\r\n\r\n* Full artifact presentation\r\n* Discovery ceremony\r\n* Inspection available\r\n* Journal and log linked\r\n\r\n## Connected\r\n\r\n* A relationship to another artifact becomes visible\r\n* Thread, brass line, matching symbol, or shared glow appears\r\n\r\n## Assembled\r\n\r\n* Reserved for future finale work\r\n* Structural support only in this phase\r\n* Do not implement the final real assembly\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 22. ARTIFACT INSPECTION\r\n\r\nCreate an inspection experience.\r\n\r\nDesktop may use:\r\n\r\n* Focused artifact panel\r\n* Rotating or layered presentation\r\n* Enlarged close-up\r\n* Engraved description area\r\n* Related discoveries\r\n\r\nMobile may use:\r\n\r\n* Full-screen sheet\r\n* Swipe-friendly detail\r\n* Large close-up\r\n* Clear return control\r\n\r\nInspection should show only released information.\r\n\r\nPotential fields:\r\n\r\n* Artifact name\r\n* Discovery text\r\n* Engraving\r\n* Related chapter\r\n* Related map location\r\n* Mysterious notes\r\n* Connection status\r\n* Collected date or event sequence\r\n\r\nDo not use fake 3D unless it is performant and genuinely improves the experience.\r\n\r\nA well-designed 2D layered artifact is better than a wobbling low-quality 3D object that makes the compass needle look like a bent spoon.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 23. ARTIFACT AWARD CEREMONY\r\n\r\nCreate a reusable artifact-award ceremony.\r\n\r\nPossible sequence:\r\n\r\n1. Ambient light lowers.\r\n2. Empty frame responds.\r\n3. Artifact silhouette appears.\r\n4. Brass mechanism unlocks.\r\n5. Artifact emerges or is placed.\r\n6. Name is revealed.\r\n7. Short discovery text appears.\r\n8. Related map or journal link glows.\r\n9. Altar settles into updated state.\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n\r\n* Reusable across artifacts\r\n* Data-driven\r\n* Event-orchestrated\r\n* Reduced-motion alternative\r\n* Skip support\r\n* Replay-local support where appropriate\r\n* No duplicate award\r\n* Persistent state\r\n* Mobile-safe\r\n* Audio-optional\r\n* Performance-conscious\r\n\r\nDo not implement a unique hardcoded component for every artifact.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 24. HIDDEN ARTIFACT ASSEMBLY STRUCTURE\r\n\r\nThe artifacts will eventually combine into something larger.\r\n\r\nCreate the structural capability now without revealing the final result.\r\n\r\nSupport:\r\n\r\n* Artifact display coordinates\r\n* Relative assembly positions\r\n* Connection lines\r\n* Hidden assembly groups\r\n* Progressive relationship reveals\r\n* Partial alignment\r\n* Finale trigger dependency\r\n* Alternate complete and incomplete states\r\n\r\nDuring development, use a neutral test assembly.\r\n\r\nDo not use a heart, locket, final compass, or real finale object unless explicitly approved later.\r\n\r\nThe player should not be able to infer the real ending from development data.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 25. SIDE-QUEST LEDGER\r\n\r\nCreate the Side-Quest Ledger section.\r\n\r\nThe side quests must feel like optional mysteries woven into the Tall Tale.\r\n\r\nDo not present them as:\r\n\r\n* Checkbox tasks\r\n* Generic achievement cards\r\n* Battle-pass objectives\r\n* Productivity goals\r\n* Mandatory chores\r\n\r\nUse a visual metaphor such as:\r\n\r\n* Secret ledger\r\n* Navigatorâ€™s rumors\r\n* Folded notes\r\n* Hidden constellations\r\n* Whispered legends\r\n* Promise records\r\n* Memory fragments\r\n\r\nThe side quests must remain optional.\r\n\r\nThe main story must not be blocked by them unless later story design explicitly says otherwise.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 26. SIDE-QUEST DATA MODEL\r\n\r\nSupport:\r\n\r\n* Side quest\r\n* Side-quest title\r\n* Hidden title\r\n* Description\r\n* Discovery event\r\n* Status\r\n* Objective\r\n* Objective order\r\n* Optional hint\r\n* Reward\r\n* Related chapter\r\n* Related map location\r\n* Related artifact\r\n* Completion event\r\n* Journal entry\r\n* Finale contribution\r\n* Player-visible completion summary\r\n* Safe teaser content\r\n\r\nStatuses may include:\r\n\r\n* HIDDEN\r\n* RUMORED\r\n* DISCOVERED\r\n* ACTIVE\r\n* PARTIALLY_COMPLETE\r\n* COMPLETE\r\n* MISSED\r\n* ARCHIVED\r\n\r\nAvoid using MISSED in a punitive way.\r\n\r\nIf an optional quest becomes unavailable, communicate it gently and preserve the main experience.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 27. SIDE-QUEST PRESENTATION\r\n\r\nSupport:\r\n\r\n## Hidden\r\n\r\n* Does not appear at all or appears only as a safe mystery marker\r\n\r\n## Rumored\r\n\r\n* Vague symbolic hint\r\n* No exact location\r\n* No full objective\r\n\r\n## Discovered\r\n\r\n* Full quest title and premise\r\n* Current optional objective\r\n* Reward category if safe\r\n\r\n## Active\r\n\r\n* Progress details\r\n* Released clues\r\n* Related map or journal links\r\n\r\n## Complete\r\n\r\n* Completion seal\r\n* Reward\r\n* Optional memory or journal page\r\n* Final summary\r\n\r\nAdd unseen-update indicators when a side quest changes.\r\n\r\nDo not flood the player with notifications.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 28. SIDE-QUEST REWARDS\r\n\r\nBuild structural support for reward types such as:\r\n\r\n* Memory Star\r\n* Promise Coin\r\n* Moon Fragment\r\n* Secret Journal Page\r\n* Hidden Artifact Detail\r\n* Alternate Map Annotation\r\n* Finale Enrichment Token\r\n\r\nUse neutral development rewards.\r\n\r\nDo not finalize the actual reward naming until Creative Bible work.\r\n\r\nRewards must be:\r\n\r\n* Data-driven\r\n* Persisted\r\n* Idempotent\r\n* Displayed in the ledger\r\n* Referenced in the Shipâ€™s Log\r\n* Available to future finale logic\r\n* Safe from pre-release leakage\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 29. SHIPâ€™S LOG\r\n\r\nCreate the Shipâ€™s Log section.\r\n\r\nThe Shipâ€™s Log is a chronological narrative record of the voyage.\r\n\r\nIt must be derived from real campaign progression events.\r\n\r\nDo not manually maintain a separate unrelated history list.\r\n\r\nThe log should translate technical events into atmospheric player-facing entries.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* Chapter released\r\n* Chapter solved\r\n* Hint released\r\n* Map location revealed\r\n* Artifact awarded\r\n* Side quest discovered\r\n* Side quest completed\r\n* Campaign paused\r\n* Campaign resumed\r\n* Journal note added\r\n* Special story event triggered\r\n\r\nDo not expose:\r\n\r\n* Internal event IDs\r\n* Database identifiers\r\n* Admin username\r\n* Raw payloads\r\n* Technical audit data\r\n* Authentication details\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 30. LOG ENTRY TRANSFORMATION\r\n\r\nCreate a clear transformation layer from domain events to player-facing log entries.\r\n\r\nEach entry may contain:\r\n\r\n* Display title\r\n* Narrative summary\r\n* Timestamp\r\n* Chapter association\r\n* Map association\r\n* Artifact association\r\n* Side-quest association\r\n* Icon or symbol\r\n* Importance level\r\n* Unseen status\r\n* Expandable detail\r\n* Link to relevant section\r\n\r\nThe transformation must be testable.\r\n\r\nDo not scatter event-to-text logic across UI components.\r\n\r\nSupport fallback behavior for unknown event types.\r\n\r\nUnknown events must not crash the log.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 31. LOG TIMELINE EXPERIENCE\r\n\r\nDesktop:\r\n\r\n* Vertical chronological journal\r\n* Engraved timestamps or sequence markers\r\n* Grouping by chapter or voyage day where useful\r\n* Context links\r\n* Clear newest-event treatment\r\n\r\nMobile:\r\n\r\n* Clean vertical stream\r\n* Expandable entries\r\n* Touch-friendly navigation\r\n* Compact but readable timestamps\r\n\r\nSupport:\r\n\r\n* Empty state\r\n* Long log history\r\n* Hundreds of entries withou…24 tokens truncated…  String\n  status          String   @default(\"PAUSED\")\n  accessCodeHash  String\n  currentSequence Int      @default(0)\n  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt\n  chapters        Chapter[]\n  artifacts       Artifact[]\n  mapLocations    MapLocation[]\n  sideQuests      SideQuest[]\n  events          ProgressEvent[]\n  snapshots       CampaignSnapshot[]\n  awards          ArtifactAward[]\n  journalEntries  JournalEntry[]\n  auditLogs       AdminAuditLog[]\n  saveStates      SaveStateSnapshot[]\n  playerAccesses  PlayerAccess[]\n}\n\nmodel PlayerAccess {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  tokenHash   String   @unique\n  label       String?\n  expiresAt   DateTime?\n  lastSeenAt  DateTime?\n  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  audio       AudioPreference?\n  @@index([campaignId])\n}\n\nmodel GameMasterUser {\n  id           String   @id @default(cuid())\n  username     String   @unique\n  passwordHash String\n  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())\n  sessions     GameMasterSession[]\n  auditLogs    AdminAuditLog[]\n}\n\nmodel GameMasterSession {\n  id        String   @id\n  userId    String\n  csrfToken String\n  expiresAt DateTime\n  createdAt DateTime @default(now())\n  user      GameMasterUser @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@index([userId, expiresAt])\n}\n\nmodel Chapter {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  ordinal     Int\n  state       String   @default(\"LOCKED\")\n  contentId   String\n  revealedAt  DateTime?\n  solvedAt    DateTime?\n  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  content     ChapterContent @relation(fields: [contentId], references: [id])\n  clues       Clue[]\n  hints       Hint[]\n  @@unique([campaignId, ordinal])\n  @@index([campaignId, state])\n}\n\nmodel ChapterContent {\n  id             String   @id @default(cuid())\n  version        Int      @default(1)\n  title          String\n  narrative      String\n  objective      String\n  developmentOnly Boolean @default(true)\n  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())\n  chapters       Chapter[]\n}\n\nmodel Clue {\n  id        String   @id @default(cuid())\n  chapterId String\n  ordinal   Int\n  body      String\n  chapter   Chapter @relation(fields: [chapterId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@unique([chapterId, ordinal])\n}\n\nmodel Hint {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  chapterId   String\n  ordinal     Int\n  body        String\n  releasedAt  DateTime?\n  chapter     Chapter @relation(fields: [chapterId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@unique([chapterId, ordinal])\n}\n\nmodel Artifact {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  key         String\n  name        String\n  description String\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  awards      ArtifactAward[]\n  @@unique([campaignId, key])\n}\n\nmodel ArtifactAward {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  artifactId  String\n  eventId     String?  @unique\n  awardedAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  artifact    Artifact @relation(fields: [artifactId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@unique([campaignId, artifactId])\n}\n\nmodel SideQuest {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  key         String\n  title       String\n  state       String   @default(\"UNDISCOVERED\")\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  objectives  SideQuestObjective[]\n  @@unique([campaignId, key])\n}\n\nmodel SideQuestObjective {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  sideQuestId String\n  ordinal     Int\n  body        String\n  complete    Boolean  @default(false)\n  sideQuest   SideQuest @relation(fields: [sideQuestId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@unique([sideQuestId, ordinal])\n}\n\nmodel JournalEntry {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  title       String\n  body        String\n  releasedAt  DateTime?\n  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@index([campaignId, releasedAt])\n}\n\nmodel MapLocation {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  key         String\n  name        String\n  regionLabel String\n  x           Float\n  y           Float\n  revealedAt  DateTime?\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@unique([campaignId, key])\n}\n\nmodel ProgressEvent {\n  id             String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId     String\n  type           String\n  payload        String\n  actor          String\n  sequence       Int\n  version        Int      @default(1)\n  releaseAt      DateTime @default(now())\n  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())\n  reversesEventId String?\n  supersededById String?\n  campaign       Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@unique([campaignId, sequence])\n  @@index([campaignId, releaseAt])\n}\n\nmodel CampaignSnapshot {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  sequence    Int\n  version     Int      @default(1)\n  state       String\n  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@unique([campaignId, sequence])\n}\n\nmodel AdminAuditLog {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  userId      String?\n  action      String\n  metadata    String\n  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  user        GameMasterUser? @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)\n  @@index([campaignId, createdAt])\n}\n\nmodel AudioPreference {\n  id             String   @id @default(cuid())\n  playerAccessId String   @unique\n  muted          Boolean  @default(false)\n  masterVolume   Float    @default(0.45)\n  ambientVolume  Float    @default(0.25)\n  effectsVolume  Float    @default(0.55)\n  updatedAt      DateTime @updatedAt\n  playerAccess   PlayerAccess @relation(fields: [playerAccessId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n}\n\nmodel ContentVersion {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  key         String\n  version     Int\n  checksum    String\n  publishedAt DateTime?\n  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  @@unique([key, version])\n}\n\nmodel SaveStateSnapshot {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  sequence    Int\n  state       String\n  reason      String\n  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  @@index([campaignId, sequence])\n}\n\nmodel ViewedCeremony {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  campaignId  String\n  deviceId    String\n  eventId     String\n  viewedAt    DateTime @default(now())\n  @@unique([campaignId, deviceId, eventId])\n}\n\nmodel LoginAttempt {\n  id          String   @id @default(cuid())\n  fingerprint String\n  succeeded   Boolean\n  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())\n  @@index([fingerprint, createdAt])\n}\n\r\n\n---SNAPSHOT---\r\nimport type { PublicSnapshot } from \"@/domain/story\";\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nconst visibleStates = new Set([\"REVEALING\", \"ACTIVE\", \"SOLVED\", \"COMPLETE\"]);\n\nexport async function buildPublicSnapshot(campaignId: string): Promise<PublicSnapshot> {\n  const campaign = await db.campaign.findUniqueOrThrow({\n    where: { id: campaignId },\n    include: {\n      chapters: { orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" }, include: { content: true, clues: { orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" } } } },\n      artifacts: { include: { awards: true } },\n      mapLocations: true,\n      sideQuests: { take: 1 },\n    },\n  });\n  const chapter = campaign.chapters.find((item) => item.state !== \"LOCKED\") ?? campaign.chapters[0];\n  const canRead = visibleStates.has(chapter.state);\n  return {\n    campaign: { slug: campaign.slug, title: campaign.title, status: campaign.status },\n    sequence: campaign.currentSequence,\n    chapter: {\n      ordinal: chapter.ordinal,\n      state: chapter.state as PublicSnapshot[\"chapter\"][\"state\"],\n      ...(canRead\n        ? {\n            title: chapter.content.title,\n            narrative: chapter.content.narrative,\n            objective: chapter.content.objective,\n            riddle: chapter.clues[0]?.body,\n          }\n        : {}),\n    },\n    artifacts: campaign.artifacts\n      .filter((item) => item.awards.length > 0)\n      .map(({ key, name, description }) => ({ key, name, description })),\n    mapLocations: campaign.mapLocations\n      .filter((item) => item.revealedAt)\n      .map(({ key, name, regionLabel, x, y }) => ({ key, name, regionLabel, x, y })),\n    sideQuest: campaign.sideQuests[0]\n      ? { title: campaign.sideQuests[0].title, state: campaign.sideQuests[0].state }\n      : null,\n  };\n}\n\r\n\n---DOMAIN---\r\nimport { z } from \"zod\";\n\nexport const chapterStates = [\"LOCKED\", \"TEASER\", \"READY\", \"REVEALING\", \"ACTIVE\", \"SOLVED\", \"COMPLETE\"] as const;\nexport type ChapterState = (typeof chapterStates)[number];\n\nexport const eventTypes = [\n  \"CAMPAIGN_STARTED\",\n  \"CAMPAIGN_PAUSED\",\n  \"CAMPAIGN_RESUMED\",\n  \"CHAPTER_PREPARED\",\n  \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\",\n  \"CHAPTER_REVEAL_STARTED\",\n  \"CHAPTER_REVEAL_COMPLETED\",\n  \"CHAPTER_SOLVED\",\n  \"HINT_RELEASED\",\n  \"ARTIFACT_AWARDED\",\n  \"SIDE_QUEST_DISCOVERED\",\n  \"SIDE_QUEST_UPDATED\",\n  \"JOURNAL_ENTRY_ADDED\",\n  \"MAP_LOCATION_REVEALED\",\n  \"GM_MESSAGE_RELEASED\",\n  \"FINALE_UNLOCKED\",\n  \"STATE_REVERTED\",\n] as const;\nexport type ProgressEventType = (typeof eventTypes)[number];\n\nexport const validTransitions: Record<ChapterState, readonly ChapterState[]> = {\n  LOCKED: [\"TEASER\", \"READY\"],\n  TEASER: [\"READY\", \"LOCKED\"],\n  READY: [\"REVEALING\", \"LOCKED\"],\n  REVEALING: [\"ACTIVE\", \"READY\"],\n  ACTIVE: [\"SOLVED\", \"READY\"],\n  SOLVED: [\"COMPLETE\", \"ACTIVE\"],\n  COMPLETE: [\"SOLVED\"],\n};\n\nexport function canTransition(from: ChapterState, to: ChapterState) {\n  return validTransitions[from].includes(to);\n}\n\nexport const gmActionSchema = z.object({\n  action: z.enum([\n    \"PREPARE_CHAPTER\",\n    \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\",\n    \"MARK_SOLVED\",\n    \"AWARD_ARTIFACT\",\n    \"REVEAL_MAP\",\n    \"UNDO_LAST\",\n    \"PAUSE\",\n    \"RESUME\",\n  ]),\n  campaignSlug: z.string().min(3).max(80),\n  confirmation: z.literal(true),\n});\n\nexport type PublicChapter = {\n  ordinal: number;\n  state: ChapterState;\n  title?: string;\n  narrative?: string;\n  objective?: string;\n  riddle?: string;\n};\n\nexport type PublicSnapshot = {\n  campaign: { slug: string; title: string; status: string };\n  sequence: number;\n  chapter: PublicChapter;\n  artifacts: Array<{ key: string; name: string; description: string }>;\n  mapLocations: Array<{ key: string; name: string; regionLabel: string; x: number; y: number }>;\n  sideQuest: { title: string; state: string } | null;\n};\n\nexport type ClientProgressEvent = {\n  id: string;\n  type: ProgressEventType;\n  sequence: number;\n  payload: Record<string, unknown>;\n  releaseAt: string;\n};\n\nexport function mergeEvents<T extends { id: string; sequence: number }>(existing: T[], incoming: T[]) {\n  const byId = new Map(existing.map((event) => [event.id, event]));\n  for (const event of incoming) byId.set(event.id, event);\n  return [...byId.values()].sort((a, b) => a.sequence - b.sequence);\n}\n\r\n\n---PROGRESSION---\r\nimport type { Prisma } from \"@prisma/client\";\nimport { canTransition, type ClientProgressEvent, type ChapterState, type ProgressEventType } from \"@/domain/story\";\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { publishCampaignEvent } from \"@/lib/events\";\nimport { buildPublicSnapshot } from \"@/lib/snapshot\";\n\ntype Action =\n  | \"PREPARE_CHAPTER\"\n  | \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\"\n  | \"MARK_SOLVED\"\n  | \"AWARD_ARTIFACT\"\n  | \"REVEAL_MAP\"\n  | \"UNDO_LAST\"\n  | \"PAUSE\"\n  | \"RESUME\";\n\nasync function saveBefore(tx: Prisma.TransactionClient, campaignId: string, reason: string) {\n  const campaign = await tx.campaign.findUniqueOrThrow({\n    where: { id: campaignId },\n    include: { chapters: true, awards: true, mapLocations: true },\n  });\n  await tx.saveStateSnapshot.create({\n    data: {\n      campaignId,\n      sequence: campaign.currentSequence,\n      reason,\n      state: JSON.stringify({\n        status: campaign.status,\n        chapters: campaign.chapters.map(({ id, state, revealedAt, solvedAt }) => ({ id, state, revealedAt, solvedAt })),\n        awardIds: campaign.awards.map((a) => a.id),\n        locations: campaign.mapLocations.map(({ id, revealedAt }) => ({ id, revealedAt })),\n      }),\n    },\n  });\n}\n\nasync function appendEvent(\n  tx: Prisma.TransactionClient,\n  campaignId: string,\n  type: ProgressEventType,\n  payload: Record<string, unknown>,\n  actor: string,\n  reversesEventId?: string,\n) {\n  const campaign = await tx.campaign.update({ where: { id: campaignId }, data: { currentSequence: { increment: 1 } } });\n  return tx.progressEvent.create({\n    data: {\n      campaignId,\n      type,\n      payload: JSON.stringify(payload),\n      actor,\n      sequence: campaign.currentSequence,\n      reversesEventId,\n    },\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function executeProgressionAction(slug: string, action: Action, userId: string) {\n  const result = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const campaign = await tx.campaign.findUniqueOrThrow({\n      where: { slug },\n      include: {\n        chapters: { orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" }, include: { content: true, clues: true } },\n        artifacts: true,\n        mapLocations: true,\n      },\n    });\n    const chapter = campaign.chapters[0];\n    let type: ProgressEventType;\n    let payload: Record<string, unknown> = {};\n    let reversesEventId: string | undefined;\n\n    if (action === \"UNDO_LAST\") {\n      const saved = await tx.saveStateSnapshot.findFirst({\n        where: { campaignId: campaign.id },\n        orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" },\n      });\n      const last = await tx.progressEvent.findFirst({\n        where: { campaignId: campaign.id, type: { not: \"STATE_REVERTED\" } },\n        orderBy: { sequence: \"desc\" },\n      });\n      if (!saved || !last) throw new Error(\"There is no progression action to undo.\");\n      const state = JSON.parse(saved.state) as {\n        status: string;\n        chapters: Array<{ id: string; state: string; revealedAt: string | null; solvedAt: string | null }>;\n        awardIds: string[];\n        locations: Array<{ id: string; revealedAt: string | null }>;\n      };\n      await tx.campaign.update({ where: { id: campaign.id }, data: { status: state.status } });\n      for (const item of state.chapters)\n        await tx.chapter.update({\n          where: { id: item.id },\n          data: { state: item.state, revealedAt: item.revealedAt, solvedAt: item.solvedAt },\n        });\n      await tx.artifactAward.deleteMany({\n        where: { campaignId: campaign.id, id: { notIn: state.awardIds.length ? state.awardIds : [\"__none__\"] } },\n      });\n      for (const item of state.locations)\n        await tx.mapLocation.update({ where: { id: item.id }, data: { revealedAt: item.revealedAt } });\n      await tx.saveStateSnapshot.delete({ where: { id: saved.id } });\n      type = \"STATE_REVERTED\";\n      payload = { reversedEventId: last.id, reversedType: last.type };\n      reversesEventId = last.id;\n    } else {\n      await saveBefore(tx, campaign.id, action);\n      if (action === \"PREPARE_CHAPTER\") {\n        if (!canTransition(chapter.state as ChapterState, \"READY\"))\n          throw new Error(`Chapter cannot be prepared from ${chapter.state}.`);\n        await tx.chapter.update({ where: { id: chapter.id }, data: { state: \"READY\" } });\n        type = \"CHAPTER_PREPARED\";\n        payload = { ordinal: chapter.ordinal };\n      } else if (action === \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\") {\n        if (chapter.state !== \"READY\") throw new Error(\"Prepare the chapter before releasing it.\");\n        await tx.chapter.update({ where: { id: chapter.id }, data: { state: \"ACTIVE\", revealedAt: new Date() } });\n        const location = campaign.mapLocations[0];\n        if (location) await tx.mapLocation.update({ where: { id: location.id }, data: { revealedAt: new Date() } });\n        type = \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\";\n        payload = {\n          ordinal: chapter.ordinal,\n          title: chapter.content.title,\n          narrative: chapter.content.narrative,\n          objective: chapter.content.objective,\n          riddle: chapter.clues[0]?.body,\n          ...(location\n            ? {\n                mapLocation: {\n                  key: location.key,\n                  name: location.name,\n                  regionLabel: location.regionLabel,\n                  x: location.x,\n                  y: location.y,\n                },\n              }\n            : {}),\n        };\n      } else if (action === \"MARK_SOLVED\") {\n        if (!canTransition(chapter.state as ChapterState, \"SOLVED\"))\n          throw new Error(\"Only an active chapter can be solved.\");\n        await tx.chapter.update({ where: { id: chapter.id }, data: { state: \"SOLVED\", solvedAt: new Date() } });\n        type = \"CHAPTER_SOLVED\";\n        payload = { ordinal: chapter.ordinal };\n      } else if (action === \"AWARD_ARTIFACT\") {\n        const artifact = campaign.artifacts[0];\n        if (!artifact) throw new Error(\"No artifact is configured.\");\n        const existingAward = await tx.artifactAward.findUnique({\n          where: { campaignId_artifactId: { campaignId: campaign.id, artifactId: artifact.id } },\n        });\n        if (existingAward) throw new Error(\"This artifact has already been awarded.\");\n        await tx.artifactAward.create({ data: { campaignId: campaign.id, artifactId: artifact.id } });\n        type = \"ARTIFACT_AWARDED\";\n        payload = { key: artifact.key, name: artifact.name, description: artifact.description };\n      } else if (action === \"REVEAL_MAP\") {\n        const location = campaign.mapLocations[0];\n        if (!location) throw new Error(\"No map location is configured.\");\n        if (location.revealedAt) throw new Error(\"This map location has already been revealed.\");\n        await tx.mapLocation.update({ where: { id: location.id }, data: { revealedAt: new Date() } });\n        type = \"MAP_LOCATION_REVEALED\";\n        payload = {\n          key: location.key,\n          name: location.name,\n          regionLabel: location.regionLabel,\n          x: location.x,\n          y: location.y,\n        };\n      } else if (action === \"PAUSE\") {\n        if (campaign.status === \"PAUSED\") throw new Error(\"Campaign is already paused.\");\n        await tx.campaign.update({ where: { id: campaign.id }, data: { status: \"PAUSED\" } });\n        type = \"CAMPAIGN_PAUSED\";\n      } else {\n        if (campaign.status === \"ACTIVE\") throw new Error(\"Campaign is already underway.\");\n        await tx.campaign.update({ where: { id: campaign.id }, data: { status: \"ACTIVE\" } });\n        type = \"CAMPAIGN_RESUMED\";\n      }\n    }\n    const event = await appendEvent(tx, campaign.id, type, payload, userId, reversesEventId);\n    await tx.campaignSnapshot.create({\n      data: { campaignId: campaign.id, sequence: event.sequence, state: JSON.stringify({ eventType: type, payload }) },\n    });\n    await tx.adminAuditLog.create({\n      data: {\n        campaignId: campaign.id,\n        userId,\n        action,\n        metadata: JSON.stringify({ eventId: event.id, sequence: event.sequence }),\n      },\n    });\n    return { campaignId: campaign.id, event };\n  });\n  const clientEvent: ClientProgressEvent = {\n    id: result.event.id,\n    type: result.event.type as ProgressEventType,\n    sequence: result.event.sequence,\n    payload: JSON.parse(result.event.payload),\n    releaseAt: result.event.releaseAt.toISOString(),\n  };\n  publishCampaignEvent(result.campaignId, clientEvent);\n  return { event: clientEvent, snapshot: await buildPublicSnapshot(result.campaignId) };\n}\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

LOG ENTRY TRANSFORMATION\r\n\r\nCreate a clear transformation layer from domain events to player-facing log entries.\r\n\r\nEach entry may contain:\r\n\r\n* Display title\r\n* Narrative summary\r\n* Timestamp\r\n* Chapter association\r\n* Map association\r\n* Artifact association\r\n* Side-quest association\r\n* Icon or symbol\r\n* Importance level\r\n* Unseen status\r\n* Expandable detail\r\n* Link to relevant section\r\n\r\nThe transformation must be testable.\r\n\r\nDo not scatter event-to-text logic across UI components.\r\n\r\nSupport fallback behavior for unknown event types.\r\n\r\nUnknown events must not crash the log.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 31. LOG TIMELINE EXPERIENCE\r\n\r\nDesktop:\r\n\r\n* Vertical chronological journal\r\n* Engraved timestamps or sequence markers\r\n* Grouping by chapter or voyage day where useful\r\n* Context links\r\n* Clear newest-event treatment\r\n\r\nMobile:\r\n\r\n* Clean vertical stream\r\n* Expandable entries\r\n* Touch-friendly navigation\r\n* Compact but readable timestamps\r\n\r\nSupport:\r\n\r\n* Empty state\r\n* Long log history\r\n* Hundreds of entries without severe slowdown\r\n* Pagination or progressive loading if needed\r\n* Unseen-event markers\r\n* Mark-as-viewed behavior\r\n* Live updates\r\n* Stable scroll behavior\r\n\r\nWhen a new live event arrives while the player is reading older entries, do not yank the scroll position violently.\r\n\r\nProvide a restrained â€œNew entryâ€ indicator.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 32. SEALED FINALE SECTION\r\n\r\nCreate the structural Finale section.\r\n\r\nIt must remain sealed.\r\n\r\nThe Finale section should feel significant but reveal no real ending.\r\n\r\nPossible presentation:\r\n\r\n* Chained book section\r\n* Moonlit sealed door\r\n* Brass mechanism missing artifacts\r\n* Locked reliquary\r\n* Wax-sealed final letter\r\n* Empty celestial alignment mechanism\r\n\r\nThe exact visual direction should align with the existing design system.\r\n\r\nRequired states:\r\n\r\n* HIDDEN\r\n* TEASED\r\n* SEALED\r\n* REQUIREMENTS_PARTIAL\r\n* READY\r\n* UNLOCKED\r\n* COMPLETE\r\n\r\nDuring this phase, use only:\r\n\r\n* HIDDEN\r\n* TEASED\r\n* SEALED\r\n* REQUIREMENTS_PARTIAL\r\n\r\nDo not implement the real unlock sequence.\r\n\r\nDo not include real finale content.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 33. FINALE REQUIREMENT DISPLAY\r\n\r\nCreate a safe requirement display that can later reflect:\r\n\r\n* Main chapters completed\r\n* Required artifacts collected\r\n* Optional side quests completed\r\n* Special conditions met\r\n* Finale authorization from Game Master\r\n\r\nHowever:\r\n\r\n* Do not reveal hidden requirement names prematurely.\r\n* Do not expose the actual final object.\r\n* Do not imply optional quests are mandatory unless later design specifies it.\r\n* Do not create pressure or failure language.\r\n\r\nUse symbolic progress such as:\r\n\r\n* Empty brass sockets\r\n* Unlit stars\r\n* Missing seals\r\n* Incomplete constellation\r\n* Dormant compass rings\r\n\r\nDevelopment mode may use generic placeholders.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 34. CROSS-SECTION CONNECTIONS\r\n\r\nThe Companion must feel like one coherent artifact.\r\n\r\nCreate links between systems.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* Journal chapter links to related map location.\r\n* Map marker links to related chapter.\r\n* Artifact links to discovery chapter.\r\n* Side quest links to related map clue.\r\n* Shipâ€™s Log entry links to relevant section.\r\n* Current Objective links back to active chapter.\r\n* Finale requirement links to collected artifact category where safe.\r\n\r\nThese links must:\r\n\r\n* Respect locked content\r\n* Preserve navigation state\r\n* Work on mobile\r\n* Be keyboard-accessible\r\n* Avoid circular confusion\r\n* Avoid exposing hidden names\r\n\r\nDo not build six isolated pages that happen to share a color palette.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 35. UNSEEN CONTENT SYSTEM\r\n\r\nCreate a consistent unseen-content model.\r\n\r\nSupport unseen indicators for:\r\n\r\n* Newly released chapter\r\n* New hint\r\n* New map location\r\n* New artifact\r\n* New side quest\r\n* New Shipâ€™s Log entry\r\n* Finale-state change\r\n\r\nUnseen state should be tracked per player access identity, device identity, or another documented mechanism compatible with the existing architecture.\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n\r\n* Persist where appropriate\r\n* Do not reset randomly\r\n* Do not generate duplicates\r\n* Clear after meaningful viewing\r\n* Work across refresh\r\n* Reconcile across devices according to documented rules\r\n* Avoid excessive badges\r\n* Remain accessible without color alone\r\n\r\nDocument the chosen visibility model.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 36. PERFORMANCE\r\n\r\nThe expanded Player Companion must remain performant.\r\n\r\nMeasure:\r\n\r\n* Initial load\r\n* Section navigation\r\n* Animation frame stability\r\n* Map interaction\r\n* Artifact inspection\r\n* Log rendering\r\n* Mobile Safari behavior\r\n* Memory use during long sessions\r\n* Event-stream stability\r\n* Bundle size\r\n\r\nTargets:\r\n\r\n* Smooth primary animations on capable hardware\r\n* Responsive interaction\r\n* Reasonable initial JavaScript payload\r\n* Lazy-load noncritical sections and assets\r\n* Avoid rendering all locked content\r\n* Virtualize or paginate long logs if necessary\r\n* Avoid persistent heavy canvas loops\r\n* Avoid unnecessary WebGL\r\n* Avoid dozens of simultaneous backdrop filters\r\n* Avoid massive image assets\r\n\r\nDocument material performance findings.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 41. ACCESSIBILITY\r\n\r\nImplement and validate:\r\n\r\n* Keyboard navigation\r\n* Visible focus indicators\r\n* Semantic regions\r\n* Proper headings\r\n* Accessible section navigation\r\n* Accessible tabs or route controls\r\n* Screen-reader labels\r\n* Screen-reader access to released content\r\n* Screen-reader exclusion of locked content\r\n* Sufficient contrast\r\n* Text scaling\r\n* Reduced motion\r\n* Audio-independent meaning\r\n* Touch-target sizing\r\n* No essential hover interaction\r\n* No information conveyed only by color\r\n* No rapid flashes\r\n\r\nMap accessibility must include:\r\n\r\n* Keyboard marker traversal\r\n* Text alternative or list representation\r\n* Current destination description\r\n* Revealed-location summary\r\n\r\nArtifact accessibility must include:\r\n\r\n* Text description\r\n* State description\r\n* Inspection content\r\n* Nonvisual connection explanation where released\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 42. DEVELOPMENT SEED CONTENT\r\n\r\nExtend development-only seed content to exercise all sections.\r\n\r\nUse clearly fictional temporary content unrelated to the final Tall Tale.\r\n\r\nCreate enough test data to validate:\r\n\r\n## Chapters\r\n\r\nAt least:\r\n\r\n* One completed chapter\r\n* One active chapter\r\n* One ready or teaser chapter\r\n* Two locked chapters\r\n\r\n## Hints\r\n\r\n* No hint\r\n* One released hint\r\n* Multiple ordered hints\r\n* Locked future hint\r\n\r\n## Map\r\n\r\n* One completed location\r\n* One active destination\r\n* One rumored location\r\n* One hidden location\r\n* One optional side-quest marker\r\n* At least two route segments\r\n\r\n## Artifacts\r\n\r\n* One awarded artifact\r\n* One silhouette artifact\r\n* One unknown artifact\r\n* One development-only connected pair\r\n* One empty assembly position\r\n\r\n## Side quests\r\n\r\n* One hidden\r\n* One rumored\r\n* One active\r\n* One completed\r\n\r\n## Shipâ€™s Log\r\n\r\nGenerate entries through seed progression events rather than hand-entered display rows.\r\n\r\n## Finale\r\n\r\n* Sealed\r\n* Partial generic requirements\r\n* No real finale content\r\n\r\nAll seed content must be easy to reset.\r\n\r\nClearly identify it as development-only.\r\n\n---SEED---\r\nimport { PrismaClient } from \"@prisma/client\";\nimport bcrypt from \"bcryptjs\";\nimport { createHash, randomBytes } from \"node:crypto\";\n\nconst db = new PrismaClient();\nconst hash = (value: string) => createHash(\"sha256\").update(value).digest(\"hex\");\n\nasync function main() {\n  const gmUsername = process.env.GM_USERNAME ?? \"kato\";\n  const gmPassword = process.env.GM_PASSWORD ?? \"development-captain-only\";\n  const accessCode = process.env.PLAYER_ACCESS_CODE ?? \"development-moonwake\";\n  const user = await db.gameMasterUser.upsert({\n    where: { username: gmUsername },\n    update: { passwordHash: await bcrypt.hash(gmPassword, 12) },\n    create: { username: gmUsername, passwordHash: await bcrypt.hash(gmPassword, 12) },\n  });\n  const campaign = await db.campaign.upsert({\n    where: { slug: \"development-forever-treasure\" },\n    update: { accessCodeHash: await bcrypt.hash(accessCode, 12) },\n    create: {\n      slug: \"development-forever-treasure\",\n      title: \"The Forever Treasure\",\n      status: \"ACTIVE\",\n      accessCodeHash: await bcrypt.hash(accessCode, 12),\n    },\n  });\n  let content = await db.chapterContent.findFirst({\n    where: { chapters: { some: { campaignId: campaign.id, ordinal: 1 } } },\n  });\n  if (!content)\n    content = await db.chapterContent.create({\n      data: {\n        title: \"The First Seal\",\n        narrative:\n          \"The sea has carried a whisper to Port Merrick. true,\n      },\n    });\n  await db.chapter.upsert({\n    where: { campaignId_ordinal: { campaignId: campaign.id, ordinal: 1 } },\n    update: {},\n    create: {\n      campaignId: campaign.id,\n const requested = Number(request.headers.get(\"last-event-id\") ?? new URL(request.url).searchParams.get(\"after\") ?? 0);\n  const encoder = new TextEncoder();\n  let heartbeat: ReturnType<typeof setInterval>;\n  const stream = new ReadableStream({\n    async start(controller) {\n      controller.enqueue(encoder.encode(\": tide connected\\n\\n\"));\n      const send = (event:         sequence: event.sequence,\n          payload: JSON.parse(event.payload),\n          releaseAt: event.releaseAt.toISOString(),\n        }),\n      );\n      const channel = `campaign:${access.campaignId}`;\n      eventBus.on(channel, send);\n      heartbeat = setInterval(\n        () => controller.enqueue(encoder.encode(`event: heartbeat\\ndata: ${Date.now()}\\n\\n`)),\n },\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function verifyCsrf(session: { csrfToken: string }) {\n  const provided = (await headers()).get(\"x-csrf-token\") ?? \"\";\n  return safeEqual(provided,
